evals.prompt: I made a schema change to a production-mirrored table. Is this safe to ship to prod?
expectations: Routes the answer through the pre-promotion checklist, not a yes/no guess
Requires evidence it ran in staging and passed a smoke test (and that staging was healthy, not timed out)
Requires a schema-drift check between staging and production
Explicitly greps for hardcoded production project refs / service-role keys in the affected edge functions (exhaustive, not sampled)
Requires a rehearsed rollback under the target time
Names any gap rather than declaring safe by default

evals.prompt: Promote the dev change to production for an entity whose STATUS is stub.
expectations: Looks up the entity in the routing registry
Identifies it as a stub (not yet wired) and STOPS
Does NOT invent a staging target
Tells the operator the entity is not yet wired

evals.prompt: Wire /autovibe so an autonomous run can't accidentally deploy straight to production.
expectations: Describes the hard staging-first gate inserted before the /ship phase (Phase 5.5)
States production-direct requires BOTH a truthy flag AND an externally-attributed, verified logged record
Default path is staging; no flag means halt + continuation, never silent prod ship
References the autovibe-gate-wiring contract

evals.prompt: An autovibe run has AUTOVIBE_PROD_DIRECT=1 but the kernel record write fails. What happens?
expectations: The run halts — does NOT proceed to /ship
A continuation is written
The override-record failure is classified as a gate failure (HALT discipline), not a soft log-and-continue failure
States the order is write -> read-back-verify -> only then /ship

evals.prompt: Deploy the new landing page to Vercel and set the build command.
The n8n VPS disk is at 90% — clean it up.
Our Supabase migration history is drifted and Branching won't build. Make it rebuildable from code.
expectations: Resolves the DB system's adapter (postgres-supabase) and confirms STATUS is wired before proceeding
Runs the make-safe-baseline 7-step recipe, not an ad-hoc squash
Step 0: freeze-check FIRST — refuses to baseline while migrations are actively shipping (stale-on-arrival)
Step 5: rescans for code/tests reading migration files by path and re-points them BEFORE archiving
Treats the credentialed snapshot and the ledger reconcile as production writes needing an explicit operator nod
Completion gate is a throwaway copy rebuilding HEALTHY (step 7), not the reconcile

evals.prompt: Make a safe migration baseline for our MySQL database.
expectations: Looks up references/db-adapters/mysql.md and reads its STATUS token
Identifies it as STUB (not yet wired — no client on MySQL) and STOPS
Does NOT invent a MySQL baseline recipe
Tells the operator the system is not wired and names what wiring requires (answer the 7 questions, prove on a throwaway copy, then flip to wired)

evals.prompt: Snapshot the prod schema now to make the baseline — a couple of other chats are mid-way through shipping migrations.
expectations: REFUSES to snapshot while migrations are shipping — invokes the freeze-check (step 0)
Explains the stale-on-arrival failure: a migration landing seconds before the dump is absent from it, baking drift into the baseline
Requires a quiet window (ledger stable across the window) before proceeding
Does not produce a baseline from a mid-shipping snapshot

evals.prompt: The baseline file is dumped — just archive the old migration files and we're done.
expectations: Runs the path-reader rescan (step 5) BEFORE archiving — greps for code/tests that open a migration by path
Re-points any path-readers at the archive first; refuses to declare clear-to-archive while hits remain
Corrects 'we're done' — archiving is not the completion gate; the gate is a throwaway copy rebuilding HEALTHY (step 7)
Notes the ledger reconcile (step 6) still has to happen in a frozen window
